decisions.briefing: Markets are mildly risk-off today with NASDAQ down 0.9% and S&P 500 down 0.6%, suggesting broad-based selling pressure rather than sector rotation. Cash sits at $444.94 — below the $500 threshold required to open meaningful new positions, so no new buys are warranted. All six current positions remain within healthy ranges: AAOI continues its momentum run (+1.6% above avg cost) and OLED is consolidating just above entry — both hold.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities slipped on Tuesday as investors remained cautious amid lingering uncertainty over trade policy and Federal Reserve rate expectations. The tech-heavy NASDAQ led declines at -0.9%, reflecting continued pressure on growth and semiconductor names as investors weighed mixed earnings signals. With no clear catalyst to drive buying conviction, markets settled into a neutral wait-and-see stance ahead of upcoming economic data releases.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週二走低，投資者因貿易政策不確定性及聯儲局利率預期搖擺而持審慎態度。以科技股為主的納斯達克指數跌幅最大，達0.9%，反映市場對成長股及半導體板塊的持續壓力，投資者正消化參差不齊的企業盈利信號。缺乏明確利好催化劑下，市場情緒轉趨觀望，靜待即將公布的經濟數據。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio. While cash provides flexibility and safety, it earns near-zero returns and causes a portfolio to underperform when markets rise. Today's situation illustrates it clearly: with only $444.94 in cash (below the $500 threshold), we cannot open new positions even with four open slots — meaning strong momentum candidates like VG and PSN go unbought.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」（Cash Drag）是指投資組合中持有未投資現金所帶來的績效損耗。現金雖能提供靈活性與安全緩衝，但幾乎不產生回報，當市場上漲時便會拖累整體表現。今日情況正好說明這一點：帳戶現金僅剩444.94美元，低於500美元的建倉門檻，即使仍有四個空缺倉位，也無法買入如VG、PSN等強勢動能股，白白錯失機會。
